overview: Grixis Reanimator is a combo deck that focuses on digging for large threats and reanimating them from the graveyard. Thoughtseize provides crucial early disruption to clear the path for your finishers. Abhorrent Oculus and Archon of Cruelty serve as primary win conditions, offering value and board control respectively.
win_conditions: Reanimate Archon of Cruelty to clear the board and deal combat damage
Cast Abhorrent Oculus to generate card advantage and find reanimation targets
Dig for and reanimate large threats to overwhelm opponents quickly

keep_criteria: Hands with Thoughtseize and a reanimation spell
Hands with two lands and a way to dig
Hands containing Abhorrent Oculus or Archon of Cruelty with a cheap instant
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one discard spell (Thoughtseize/Duress), and a reanimation spell or early threat.
Prioritize hands that can play turn 1 Thoughtseize into a land or early threat, ensuring you can sequence mana for disruption before committing to the graveyard.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with no disruption or reanimation
Hands with three or more lands
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, only lands, or only threats with no discard/mana.

early_game: Play Thoughtseize to remove key threats or combo pieces
Cast reanimation spells to bring back large threats
Set up the board for a fast finish
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize targeting the opponent’s key early piece (ramp spell, combo piece, or early threat).
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a second discard or an early threat/reanimation spell.
If you have a reanimation target in hand or graveyard, sequence mana to cast it.
Land sequencing: Prioritize basics or duals that match your first two spells
mid_game: Use Abhorrent Oculus to generate card advantage and find answers
Disrupt opponent's game plan with hand traps
Balance mana for reanimation versus disruption
avoid overcommitting to the graveyard without protection.
Keep cards in hand for interaction, and use Abhorrent Oculus to dig for reanimation spells or threats.
Track your graveyard carefully to ensure you have enough resources to follow up on reanimation plays.
Commit threats when you have protection (discard, counters, or opponent tapped out) or when you can follow up with additional value.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or targeted removal in play.
late_game: Leverage Archon of Cruelty's combat trick to clear the board
Use Abhorrent Oculus's value engine to outlast opponents
Reanimate multiple threats to secure victory
Reanimated Archon of Cruelty or Abhorrent Oculus.
Oculus generates card advantage and mana to swing for lethal, while Archon crashes through with trample/haste and clears the board on entry.
Secure these threats in the graveyard, protect them from hate, and swing when the path is clear.
Go all-in when you have protection, the opponent is tapped out, the graveyard is safe from hate, or you can swing for lethal in one or two turns.
Also commit when you’ve disrupted the opponent’s hand and they have no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Abhorrent Oculus
key_cards.notes: Use its triggered abilities to dig for reanimation targets or answers while generating life gain and card draw to stay ahead.

key_cards.name: Archon of Cruelty
key_cards.notes: Reanimate it to gain a massive combat trick that can remove opposing threats while dealing significant damage to the opponent.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Cast it on turn one to remove key combo pieces, threats, or answers, setting up a clear path for your reanimation strategy.

sequencing_tips: Hold Thoughtseize for the opponent's most impactful card rather than using it preemptively
Reanimate Archon of Cruelty when the opponent has multiple threats to maximize its combat trick
Play Abhorrent Oculus when you need to dig for a specific reanimation target or answer
Prioritize reanimating threats that can immediately impact the board state
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the graveyard without protection, forgetting to track the opponent’s hand for counters/removal, and missequencing mana for reanimation.
Abhorrent Oculus can be reanimated to draw cards and generate mana, enabling further plays, while Archon of Cruelty’s enter-the-battlefield effect can clear the board.
Always keep a Thoughtseize for turn 1 to disrupt the early game, and be prepared to pivot if your primary game plan is shut down.

matchups.advice: Use Thoughtseize to remove key finishers and reanimate large blockers or finishers quickly.
Race them by reanimating threats before they stabilize and use Abhorrent Oculus to generate value.
Disrupt their key threats with Thoughtseize and reanimate Archon of Cruelty to clear their board.

Use discard to remove counterspells before casting reanimation spells. Cast threats in response to their interaction, and use cheap spells to pressure. If counters are pervasive, pivot to graveyard value or alternative win conditions.
Apply early pressure with discard and early threats. Don’t let them stabilize or find their big threats. Use graveyard hate if they rely on it, and reanimate threats to race their ramp spells.

Win the discard war by prioritizing graveyard hate and tracking their reanimation spells. Reanimate threats when the opponent is tapped out, and use Abhorrent Oculus to out-value them in card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Fatal Push), graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void), and combo win conditions.
Note when the opponent taps out for interaction, as this is your window to reanimate threats or dig deeper.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards dictate primary win paths
